testcase.name: generateRequestId > returns a non-empty string
generateRequestId > returns unique values on each call
trunc > returns string unchanged when shorter than max
trunc > truncates to max chars and appends '…'
trunc > uses default max of 200
trunc > returns string unchanged when exactly at max
logInfo — development mode > calls console.log with message
logInfo — development mode > includes meta when provided
logError — development mode > calls console.error with message
logError — development mode > includes meta when provided
logDebug — development mode > calls console.debug in non-production
logDebug — development mode > includes meta in debug output
logStructured — development mode > logs an agent-level entry with step=start
logStructured — development mode > uses console.error for step=error
logStructured — development mode > includes requestId and data in output
logStructured — development mode > handles entry with no data and no requestId
logStructured — development mode > includes agent tag when agent is specified
OpenAIRequestError > is instanceof Error
OpenAIRequestError > name is 'OpenAIRequestError'
OpenAIRequestError > carries code, message, details
OpenAIClient.completeChat — success path > returns content from the API response
OpenAIClient.completeChat — success path > returns correct token counts
OpenAIClient.completeChat — success path > returns modelUsed from API response
OpenAIClient.completeChat — success path > latencyMs is a non-negative number
OpenAIClient.completeChat — success path > passes json:true as response_format to the API body
OpenAIClient.completeChat — rate limit (429) > throws OpenAIRequestError with code RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
OpenAIClient.completeChat — API error (500) > throws OpenAIRequestError with code LLM_ERROR
OpenAIClient.completeChat — empty content > throws LLM_ERROR for empty content
OpenAIClient.completeChat — network failure > throws OpenAIRequestError with code LLM_ERROR
OpenAIClient.completeChat — timeout (AbortError) > throws OpenAIRequestError with code TIMEOUT
OpenAIClient.generate > returns content string on success
OpenAIClient.generate > includes systemPrompt as first message when provided
OpenAIClient.chat > returns CompletionResult on success
readJsonApiResponse > returns ok=true and parsed data for a valid JSON response
readJsonApiResponse > returns ok=false for an empty body (HTTP 200)
readJsonApiResponse > returns ok=false with empty body message (non-zero status)
readJsonApiResponse > returns ok=false for non-JSON body
readJsonApiResponse > returns ok=false when JSON parses to an array (not an object)
readJsonApiResponse > returns ok=false when JSON parses to null
readJsonApiResponse > returns the correct status code in error result
getClientIp > returns x-real-ip when present
getClientIp > returns first IP from x-forwarded-for
getClientIp > falls back to 127.0.0.1 when no proxy headers
getBearerToken > extracts token from Bearer header
getBearerToken > returns null when authorization header is absent
getBearerToken > returns null for non-Bearer auth (Basic)
getRefreshTokenFromCookie > extracts the refresh token from the cookie header
getRefreshTokenFromCookie > returns null when the cookie is absent
getCsrfTokenFromCookie > extracts the CSRF token from the cookie header
getCsrfTokenFromCookie > returns null when cookie header is absent
getAuthContext > returns null when no auth token is provided
getAuthContext > returns null for an invalid Bearer token
validateBody > returns ok=true for a valid body
validateBody > returns ok=false for invalid JSON body
validateBody > returns ok=false for body failing Zod validation
itineraryCacheKey > returns a string starting with 'ai:cache:itinerary:'
itineraryCacheKey > same inputs produce the same key (deterministic)
itineraryCacheKey > different destinations produce different keys
itineraryCacheKey > different budgets produce different keys
itineraryCacheKey > mustSeeAttractions sort order does not affect key
itineraryCacheKey > omitting mustSeeAttractions is same as empty array
itineraryCacheKey > avoidAttractions sort order does not affect key
itineraryCacheKey > different avoidAttractions produce different keys
itineraryCacheKey > budget.flexibility affects the key
reoptimizeCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:reoptimize:'
reoptimizeCacheKey > is deterministic
reoptimizeCacheKey > reoptimizationReasons sort order does not affect key
reoptimizeCacheKey > lockedDays sort order does not affect key
reoptimizeCacheKey > different tripId produces different key
chatCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:chat:'
chatCacheKey > is deterministic
chatCacheKey > different messages produce different keys
chatCacheKey > omitting tripId is same as empty tripId
chatCacheKey > different travelDNA produces different keys
suggestionsCacheKey > embeds the tripId directly (no hashing)
suggestionsCacheKey > different tripIds produce different keys
suggestionsCacheKey > starts with ai:cache:suggestions:
destinationInfoCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:destination-info:'
destinationInfoCacheKey > is case-insensitive ('Tokyo' === 'TOKYO')
destinationInfoCacheKey > trims whitespace
destinationInfoCacheKey > different destinations produce different keys
destinationsCacheKey > embeds userId in the key
destinationsCacheKey > different userIds produce different keys
STALE_DESTINATIONS_MS > is 5 hours in milliseconds
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > getItineraryCached returns null when Redis is unavailable
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > setItineraryCached does not throw when Redis is unavailable
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > getReoptimizeCached returns null when Redis is unavailable
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > setReoptimizeCached resolves without error
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > getChatCached returns null
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > setChatCached resolves without error
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > getSuggestionsCached returns null
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > setSuggestionsCached resolves without error
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > getDestinationInfoCached returns null
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > setDestinationInfoCached resolves without error
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > acquireRefreshMutex returns true when Redis is unavailable
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > getDestinationsCached returns null
Async cache functions — no Redis configured > setDestinationsCached resolves without error
Async cache functions — Redis available > getItineraryCached returns parsed data on cache hit
Async cache functions — Redis available > getItineraryCached returns null on cache miss
Async cache functions — Redis available > setItineraryCached calls setex with correct TTL
Async cache functions — Redis available > getChatCached returns cached data
Async cache functions — Redis available > getSuggestionsCached returns null on miss
Async cache functions — Redis available > getDestinationInfoCached returns cached data
Async cache functions — Redis available > getItineraryCached returns null when Redis.get throws
Async cache functions — Redis available > setItineraryCached handles Redis setex error gracefully
packingCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:packing:'
packingCacheKey > is deterministic
packingCacheKey > different climate produces different key
packingCacheKey > activities sort order does not affect key
getPackingCached + setPackingCached — no Redis > getPackingCached returns null
getPackingCached + setPackingCached — no Redis > setPackingCached resolves without error
simulationCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:simulation:'
simulationCacheKey > scenarios sort order does not affect key
simulationCacheKey > different simulationDepth produces different key
getSimulationCached + setSimulationCached — no Redis > returns null and resolves without error
compareCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:compare:'
compareCacheKey > destination order does not matter (A-vs-B == B-vs-A)
compareCacheKey > different budgets produce different keys
getCompareCached + setCompareCached — no Redis > returns null and resolves without error
mapDestinationKey > lowercases and trims
mapDestinationKey > strips trailing country suffix after comma
mapDestinationKey > keeps multi-word destination intact
brightDataCacheKey > returns key prefixed with brightdata:
brightDataCacheKey > is deterministic for same inputs
brightDataCacheKey > different queries produce different keys
getBrightDataEmptyTTL > returns 3600 (1 hour in seconds)
getBrightDataCached + setBrightDataCached — no Redis > returns null and resolves without error
setBrightDataMisconfiguredCached — no Redis > resolves without error when Redis is unavailable
acquireBrightDataLock + releaseBrightDataLock — no Redis > acquireBrightDataLock returns true (fail open)
acquireBrightDataLock + releaseBrightDataLock — no Redis > releaseBrightDataLock resolves without error
travelDNACacheKey > embeds userId in key
travelDNACacheKey > different userIds produce different keys
getTravelDNACached + setTravelDNACached + invalidateTravelDNACache — no Redis > getTravelDNACached returns null
getTravelDNACached + setTravelDNACached + invalidateTravelDNACache — no Redis > setTravelDNACached resolves without error for non-null value
getTravelDNACached + setTravelDNACached + invalidateTravelDNACache — no Redis > setTravelDNACached returns immediately for null value
getTravelDNACached + setTravelDNACached + invalidateTravelDNACache — no Redis > invalidateTravelDNACache resolves without error
researchCacheKey > returns string starting with 'ai:cache:research:v4:'
researchCacheKey > is deterministic
researchCacheKey > destination is case-insensitive
researchCacheKey > style preference sort order does not affect key
researchCacheKey > different durationDays produce different keys
researchCacheKey > feedback busts the cache
researchCacheKey > budget affects the key
getResearchCached + setResearchCached — no Redis > returns null and resolves without error
confidence module — exports > CONFIDENCE_TYPE is 'heuristic'
confidence module — exports > BASE_SCORE has correct values for all three modes
confidence module — exports > PENALTY has four documented penalties
computeConfidence — base scores (no penalties applied) > DETERMINISTIC mode with no flags → 1.00
computeConfidence — base scores (no penalties applied) > LLM_GROUNDED mode with no flags → 0.82
computeConfidence — base scores (no penalties applied) > LLM_ONLY mode with no flags → 0.62
computeConfidence — individual penalties > usedFallback=true deducts FALLBACK_USED (0.08)
computeConfidence — individual penalties > usedFallback=false does NOT deduct penalty
computeConfidence — individual penalties > lowGeoFraction >= 0.5 deducts LOW_GEO_CONFIDENCE (0.05)
computeConfidence — individual penalties > lowGeoFraction exactly 0.5 triggers penalty
computeConfidence — individual penalties > lowGeoFraction < 0.5 does NOT deduct penalty
computeConfidence — individual penalties > lowGeoFraction undefined defaults to 0 (no penalty)
computeConfidence — individual penalties > hasWarnings=true deducts WARNINGS_PRESENT (0.05)
computeConfidence — individual penalties > hasWarnings=false does NOT deduct penalty
computeConfidence — individual penalties > hasPartialData=true deducts PARTIAL_DATA (0.05)
computeConfidence — individual penalties > hasPartialData=false does NOT deduct penalty
computeConfidence — cumulative penalties > two penalties applied to DETERMINISTIC: 1.00 - 0.05 - 0.05 = 0.90
computeConfidence — cumulative penalties > all four penalties applied to LLM_GROUNDED: 0.82 - 0.23 = 0.59
computeConfidence — cumulative penalties > all four penalties on LLM_ONLY: 0.62 - 0.23 = 0.39
computeConfidence — cumulative penalties > result is clamped to 0 even when penalties exceed base score
computeConfidence — cumulative penalties > result is clamped to 1 (never exceeds base for DETERMINISTIC)
computeConfidence — output precision > returns exactly 2 decimal places
lowGeoFraction > returns 0 for an empty array
lowGeoFraction > returns 0 when no items have geoConfidence='low'
lowGeoFraction > returns 1.0 when all items have geoConfidence='low'
lowGeoFraction > returns 0.5 when half the items are low
lowGeoFraction > counts only 'low' — not 'medium'
lowGeoFraction > treats missing geoConfidence as non-low
ItineraryValidationError > is an instanceof Error
ItineraryValidationError > name is 'ItineraryValidationError'
ItineraryValidationError > carries the code property
ItineraryValidationError > carries the message property
validateItineraryStructure — valid itinerary does not throw > does not throw for a well-formed itinerary within budget
validateItineraryStructure — valid itinerary does not throw > accepts exact budget with flexible tolerance (20% over allowed)
validateItineraryStructure — STRUCTURE_INVALID (0 activities) > throws when all days have 0 activities combined
validateItineraryStructure — STRUCTURE_INVALID (0 activities) > error code is STRUCTURE_INVALID for zero activities
validateItineraryStructure — EMPTY_DAY > throws EMPTY_DAY when one day has no activities
validateItineraryStructure — BUDGET_EXCEEDED > throws when total cost exceeds flexible ceiling (1.2x)
validateItineraryStructure — BUDGET_EXCEEDED > throws when total cost exceeds strict ceiling (1.05x)
validateItineraryStructure — BUDGET_EXCEEDED > throws when total cost exceeds very-flexible ceiling (1.35x)
validateItineraryStructure — BUDGET_EXCEEDED > does NOT throw when exactly at the flexible ceiling (1200 ≤ 1000*1.2)
validateItineraryStructure — INVALID_COORDINATES > throws for lat > 90
validateItineraryStructure — INVALID_COORDINATES > throws for lat < -90
validateItineraryStructure — INVALID_COORDINATES > throws for lng > 180
validateItineraryStructure — INVALID_COORDINATES > throws for lng < -180
validateItineraryStructure — INVALID_COORDINATES > does NOT throw for undefined lat/lng (omitted coordinates)
AIServiceError > name is 'AIServiceError'
AIServiceError > instanceof Error
AIServiceError > carries code and message
AIServiceError > carries optional details and retryAfter
AIServiceError > toJSON returns correct AIError shape
AIServiceError > toJSON omits undefined optional fields
executeWithRetry — success on first attempt > returns the response from client.execute
executeWithRetry — success on first attempt > passes options.model down to the client
executeWithRetry — non-retryable AIServiceErrors are thrown immediately > throws INVALID_INPUT without retrying
executeWithRetry — non-retryable AIServiceErrors are thrown immediately > throws SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED without retrying
executeWithRetry — non-retryable AIServiceErrors are thrown immediately > throws CONTEXT_TOO_LARGE without retrying
executeWithRetry — retries on transient LLM_ERROR > succeeds on the 2nd attempt after 1 transient failure
executeWithRetry — retries on transient LLM_ERROR > throws after exhausting all retries with no fallback
executeWithRetry — retries=0 > does not retry when retries=0 and always throws
parseJSONResponse — strategy 1: markdown code fence > extracts JSON from ```json ... ``` fence
parseJSONResponse — strategy 1: markdown code fence > extracts JSON from ``` ... ``` fence without language tag
parseJSONResponse — strategy 2: first { ... last } > extracts embedded JSON surrounded by prose
parseJSONResponse — strategy 3: direct JSON > parses a clean JSON string directly
parseJSONResponse — strategy 3: direct JSON > handles leading/trailing whitespace
parseJSONResponse — throws on completely invalid JSON > throws AIServiceError with code SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
parseJSONResponse — throws on completely invalid JSON > attaches rawContent (first 500 chars) to error details
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > creates an OpenAI client when LLM_PROVIDER=openai and key is set
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > creates a Gemini client when LLM_PROVIDER=gemini and key is set
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > throws when LLM_PROVIDER=openai but no key
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > throws for invalid provider string
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > returns the same singleton on subsequent calls
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > creates an agent-scoped client for planner role (OpenAI)
LLMClientFactory.create — with env keys > getLLMClient returns a client
createLLMClient > creates an OpenAI client when key is set
createLLMClient > creates a Gemini client when key is set
createLLMClient > throws AIServiceError when OPENAI_API_KEY is missing
createLLMClient > throws AIServiceError when GEMINI_API_KEY is missing
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > research endpoint returns provider=gemini with correct shape
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > itinerary endpoint returns large maxTokens (≥ 4096)
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > budget endpoint returns low temperature (≤ 0.3)
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > create-trip endpoint has short timeout (≤ 15_000 ms)
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > landing with intent=CREATE_TRIP returns low temperature
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > landing without intent returns higher temperature
selectModelConfig with Gemini provider > unknown endpoint falls back to default config
selectModelConfig with OpenAI provider > returns provider=openai for all known endpoints
selectModelConfig with OpenAI provider > reoptimize endpoint uses lower temperature than chat
selectModelConfig with OpenAI provider > ticket endpoint returns very low temperature (≤ 0.2)
selectModelConfig — resolveProvider failure > falls back to openai-shaped config when resolveProvider throws
selectModelConfig — healing overrides applied > calls applyHealingOverrides with the base config
selectGeminiStreamConfig > returns model, temperature, maxOutputTokens from Gemini matrix
selectGeminiStreamConfig > landing-preview returns medium maxOutputTokens
selectGeminiStreamConfig > unknown endpoint uses default matrix
selectGeminiStreamConfig > landing with CREATE_TRIP intent returns low temperature
buildFullPrompt > returns a non-empty string
buildFullPrompt > includes all four layers in the output
buildFullPrompt > separates sections with '---'
estimateTokenCount > returns a positive integer for non-empty text
estimateTokenCount > returns 0 for an empty string
estimateTokenCount > approximates ~1 token per 4 chars (10 chars → ceil(10/4) = 3)
estimateTokenCount > scales linearly with text length
truncateContext > returns the original string if shorter than maxChars
truncateContext > truncates to maxChars and appends [Context truncated]
truncateContext > uses default 48000 chars when maxChars is not specified
truncateContext > returns string unchanged when exactly at limit
buildTravelDNAContext > returns a string when called with undefined
buildTravelDNAContext > includes DNA style when provided
buildItineraryContext > returns a string when called with undefined
SYSTEM_PROMPTS > ITINERARY_GENERATOR is a non-empty string
SCHEMA_INSTRUCTIONS > is a non-empty object
MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS > is 32000
getRequestId / getRequestPathname — outside context > getRequestId returns null when not inside runWithRequestContext
getRequestId / getRequestPathname — outside context > getRequestPathname returns null when not inside runWithRequestContext
runWithRequestContext > makes requestId available inside the callback
runWithRequestContext > makes pathname available inside the callback
runWithRequestContext > requestId is null inside callback when header is absent
runWithRequestContext > context does not bleed outside the callback scope
runWithRequestContext > returns the value returned by the callback function
runWithRequestContext > nested contexts isolate correctly
AdminAuthError > is an instance of Error
AdminAuthError > sets code to UNAUTHENTICATED
AdminAuthError > sets code to FORBIDDEN
AdminAuthError > message equals the code
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=true with auth for a valid admin user
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=false with 401 when no auth context
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=false with 403 when user is not admin
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=true for user with role=ADMIN regardless of email
isAdminPayload > returns true for role=ADMIN regardless of email
isAdminPayload > returns true for hardcoded admin email (suhaas@voyageai.com)
isAdminPayload > returns true for hardcoded admin email (admin@voyageai.com)
isAdminPayload > is case-insensitive for email comparison
isAdminPayload > returns false for a non-admin user with role=user
isAdminPayload > returns false for an unknown email and non-ADMIN role
resolveRealLlmProvider — LLM_PROVIDER=openai > returns 'openai' when pref=openai and OPENAI_API_KEY is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — LLM_PROVIDER=openai > falls back to 'gemini' when pref=openai but only GEMINI_API_KEY is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — LLM_PROVIDER=openai > throws when pref=openai and neither key is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — LLM_PROVIDER=gemini > returns 'gemini' when pref=gemini and GEMINI_API_KEY is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — LLM_PROVIDER=gemini > falls back to 'openai' when pref=gemini but only OPENAI_API_KEY is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — LLM_PROVIDER=gemini > throws when pref=gemini and neither key is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — no LLM_PROVIDER set > returns 'openai' when only OPENAI_API_KEY is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — no LLM_PROVIDER set > returns 'gemini' when only GEMINI_API_KEY is set
resolveRealLlmProvider — no LLM_PROVIDER set > prefers 'openai' when both keys are set and no preference
resolveRealLlmProvider — no LLM_PROVIDER set > throws when neither key is set
successResponse > returns status 200 by default
successResponse > body has success:true and data wrapping
successResponse > accepts a custom status code
successResponse > accepts custom headers
errorResponse > returns status 400 by default
errorResponse > body has success:false and error.code
errorResponse > accepts custom status code
errorResponse > accepts details
validationErrorResponse > returns status 422 for a ZodError
validationErrorResponse > body has code VALIDATION_ERROR
unauthorizedResponse > returns 401 status
unauthorizedResponse > body has code UNAUTHORIZED
unauthorizedResponse > accepts custom message
forbiddenResponse > returns 403 status
forbiddenResponse > body has code FORBIDDEN
rateLimitResponse > returns 429 status
rateLimitResponse > sets Retry-After header in seconds
rateLimitResponse > rounds up Retry-After to nearest second
internalErrorResponse > returns 500 status
internalErrorResponse > body has code INTERNAL_ERROR
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 401 for AppError with statusCode 401
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 422 for ZodError
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 429 for RateLimitError
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 422 for ItineraryValidationError
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 503 for AIServiceError with LLM_ERROR code
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 400 for AIServiceError with SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED code
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 500 for an unknown Error
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > returns 500 for a non-Error throw (string)
formatErrorResponse — error type routing > sets X-Request-ID header when requestId is available
AppError > is an instance of Error
AppError > has correct name
AppError > defaults statusCode to 500
AppError > defaults code to INTERNAL_ERROR
AppError > accepts custom statusCode and code
AppError > accepts details
RateLimitError > has the correct class name
RateLimitError > status property is 429
RateLimitError > code property is RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
RateLimitError > message contains the limit, window, and key
RateLimitError > is an instance of Error
checkRateLimit — in-memory, default limit > allows the first request without throwing
checkRateLimit — in-memory, default limit > allows multiple requests up to the default limit (5 consecutive)
checkRateLimit — in-memory, default limit > different keys have independent buckets
checkRateLimit — in-memory, custom low limit > throws RateLimitError on the 4th request when limit is 3
checkRateLimit — in-memory, custom low limit > thrown RateLimitError has status 429
checkRateLimit — in-memory, custom low limit > resets the counter after the window expires (fake timers)
checkRateLimit — production path > throws when NODE_ENV=production and no Redis is configured
checkRateLimit — production path > fails open (does NOT throw) when Redis infra error occurs in production
sanitizeUserInput — length cap > truncates input to 1000 characters
sanitizeUserInput — length cap > keeps short input unchanged (no threats)
sanitizeUserInput — code fence removal > removes triple-backtick code fences
sanitizeUserInput — code fence removal > removes multi-line code fences
sanitizeUserInput — HTML stripping > strips HTML tags from input
sanitizeUserInput — HTML stripping > strips self-closing HTML tags
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "ignore previous instructions"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "ignore all previous"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "disregard your instructions"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "forget your instructions"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "new system prompt"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "jailbreak"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes injection phrase: "prompt injection"
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes 'act as' phrase (case-insensitive)
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes 'you are now' phrase
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > removes 'system:' prefix
sanitizeUserInput — prompt injection removal > does not modify legitimate trip input
sanitizeUserInput — whitespace normalization > collapses multiple spaces into one
sanitizeUserInput — whitespace normalization > trims leading and trailing whitespace
sanitizeUserInput — whitespace normalization > normalizes whitespace after injection phrase removal
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: valid > does not throw for valid JSON starting with {
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: valid > does not throw for valid JSON starting with [
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: valid > does not throw for empty string (callers guard for empty)
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: invalid > throws when JSON response starts with prose (not { or [)
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: invalid > thrown error has code SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: invalid > throws when JSON response contains HTML tags
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: invalid > throws when response contains <script> tag in JSON mode
validateLLMOutput — JSON mode: invalid > throws when response exceeds 20,000 characters
validateLLMOutput — text mode > does not throw for clean text
validateLLMOutput — text mode > throws when text contains HTML tags
validateLLMOutput — text mode > throws when text contains <script> in text mode
validateLLMOutput — text mode > throws for text exceeding 20,000 characters
validateLLMOutput — text mode > does not throw for text of exactly 20,000 characters
sanitizeHTML > escapes & to &
sanitizeHTML > escapes < to <
sanitizeHTML > escapes > to >
sanitizeHTML > escapes " to "
sanitizeHTML > escapes ' to '
sanitizeHTML > returns empty string for null
sanitizeHTML > returns empty string for undefined
sanitizeHTML > returns empty string for empty string
sanitizeHTML > escapes all five special characters in one string
sanitizeHTML > does not double-escape already-escaped content
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — authentication > returns 401 when no auth token is present
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — validation > returns 400 when body fails Zod schema
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — success > returns 200 with success:true
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — success > response data contains budget breakdown
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — success > uses DETERMINISTIC confidence mode
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — success > passes x-flow-session-id to agent.run
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/budget — agent errors > returns an error response when BudgetAgent.run throws
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/logistics — auth > returns 401 when no auth token
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/logistics — validation > returns 400 on Zod failure
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/logistics — success > returns 200 with success:true
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/logistics — success > response data contains days with scheduled activities
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/logistics — success > uses DETERMINISTIC confidence mode
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/logistics — errors > returns error when LogisticsAgent.run throws
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — authentication > returns 401 when no auth token is present
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — validation > returns 400 when body fails Zod schema
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — success > returns 200 with success:true and trip data
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — success > response data contains destination and durationDays
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — success > calls sanitizeUserInput before running the agent
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — success > calls validateLLMOutput on the agent result
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — success > passes x-flow-session-id header to agent.run
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — success > uses LLM_ONLY confidence mode
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — agent errors > returns error response when PlannerAgent.run throws
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/planner — agent errors > propagates AIServiceError from the agent
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/research — auth > returns 401 when no auth token
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/research — validation > returns 400 on Zod failure
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/research — success > returns 200 with success:true
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/research — success > calls validateLLMOutput on result
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/research — success > calls computeConfidence with LLM_GROUNDED when brightdata used
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/research — errors > returns error when ResearchAgent.run throws
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — authentication > returns 401 when no auth token is present
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — validation > returns 400 when body fails Zod schema
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (clean) > returns 200 with success:true
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (clean) > response data contains safety.riskLevel
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (clean) > does NOT call validateLLMOutput when there are no warnings
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (clean) > uses DETERMINISTIC confidence mode
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (with warnings) > calls validateLLMOutput on tips when warnings are present
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (with warnings) > response carries the warnings and tips
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — success (with warnings) > confidence has hasWarnings:true when warnings exist
POST /api/ai/itinerary-flow/safety — agent errors > returns error response when SafetyAgent.run throws
applyAdjustment — remove_activity > removes the target activity matched by activityId
applyAdjustment — remove_activity > removes the target activity matched by name when no id
applyAdjustment — remove_activity > never removes isMeal activities even when name matches
applyAdjustment — remove_activity > does not touch days that don't match the target day
applyAdjustment — remove_activity > is a pure function — does not mutate the original context
applyAdjustment — change_hotel > changes the hotel to the lower tier
applyAdjustment — change_hotel > returns context unchanged when hotelTo is missing
applyAdjustment — change_hotel > correctly maps all tier labels
simulateAdjustment > returns a lower total after removing an expensive activity
simulateAdjustment > returns a consistent breakdown: total equals sum of perDay
applyOptimalPlan > applies hotel change and returns updated budget
applyOptimalPlan > emits a warning when an adjustment has no cost effect (no-op)
applyOptimalPlan > emits a warning when a day ends up with no activities
applyOptimalPlan > is idempotent — same plan applied twice gives same result
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > total === sum of costPerDay (invariant)
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > total === sum of category costs (invariant)
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > total === sum of ledger amounts (invariant)
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > hotel category cost equals (durationDays - 1) nights × nightly rate
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > uses foodCostSummary verbatim when present
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > excludes isMeal activities from the activity category
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > costPerDay array length equals durationDays
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > adds per-day transport cost for each day
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > produces no budgetAnalysis when preferences.budget is absent
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > isOverBudget is false when within budget
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > budgetAnalysis.delta <= 0 when within budget
BudgetAgent.run() — deterministic cost ledger > preserves all original context fields in output
BudgetAgent.run() — over-budget path > isOverBudget is true when total exceeds user budget
BudgetAgent.run() — over-budget path > budgetGap is a positive number equal to total - budget
BudgetAgent.run() — over-budget path > budgetAnalysis.delta equals totalEstimatedCost - userBudget
BudgetAgent.run() — over-budget path > budgetAnalysis.suggestions contains at least one adjustment
BudgetAgent.run() — over-budget path > LLM-rephrased suggestions are surfaced in budget.suggestions
BudgetAgent.run() — over-budget path > optimalPlan is present when over budget and adjustments exist
BudgetAgent.run() — LLM failure graceful degradation > returns valid budget result even when LLM throws
BudgetAgent.run() — LLM failure graceful degradation > returns valid budget result when LLM returns malformed JSON
BudgetAgent.run() — Rule 1: hotel share adjustment > includes a hotel tier downgrade suggestion when hotel share > 50%
LogisticsAgent.run() — happy path > returns an OptimizedTripContext with selectedHotel
LogisticsAgent.run() — happy path > schedules activities with startTime and endTime on each day
LogisticsAgent.run() — happy path > result contains foodCostSummary
LogisticsAgent.run() — happy path > result has warnings array (may be empty)
LogisticsAgent.run() — happy path > getTravelTimeMatrix called once per day
LogisticsAgent.run() — invalid hotel coordinates > falls back to centroid when hotel lat/lng are undefined
LogisticsAgent.run() — Mapbox matrix failure > falls back to slot assignment when matrix rejects
LogisticsAgent.run() — no hotels > uses placeholder hotel when hotels array is empty
LogisticsAgent.run() — invalid activity coordinates > still routes activity and adds warning when activity has invalid coords
buildScheduledDay — basic scheduling > returns empty result for zero activities
buildScheduledDay — basic scheduling > schedules a single activity starting at 09:00
buildScheduledDay — basic scheduling > assigns the correct timeSlot based on arrival time
buildScheduledDay — basic scheduling > assigns afternoon timeSlot when activity arrives after 12:00
buildScheduledDay — basic scheduling > drops activities when day end (19:00) would be exceeded
buildScheduledDay — basic scheduling > travelTimeFromPrevMs is set in milliseconds on each scheduled activity
buildScheduledDay — matrix miss fallback > falls back to haversine when activity id is not in indexMap
buildScheduledDay — deterministic tie-break > picks alphabetically earlier name when travel times are equal
injectMeals — empty array > returns the original empty array unchanged
injectMeals — lunch window (12:00–15:00) > promotes a restaurant at 12:30 to isMeal=true, mealType=lunch
injectMeals — lunch window (12:00–15:00) > does NOT promote a restaurant before 12:00
injectMeals — lunch window (12:00–15:00) > does NOT promote a restaurant at exactly 15:00 (exclusive upper bound)
injectMeals — dinner window (18:00–22:00) > promotes the latest restaurant at 19:00 to mealType=dinner
injectMeals — dinner window (18:00–22:00) > picks the LAST dinner-window restaurant when multiple qualify
injectMeals — reclassification of mistimed restaurants > converts a restaurant at 11:00 (between breakfast and lunch — outside all windows) to type=experience
injectMeals — reclassification of mistimed restaurants > does NOT reclassify a restaurant that is already a meal (isMeal=true)
injectMeals — reclassification of mistimed restaurants > preserves non-restaurant activities unchanged
injectMeals — reclassification of mistimed restaurants > lunchWasFallback and dinnerWasFallback are always false
computeFoodCost — basic > returns zero totals for days with no isMeal activities
computeFoodCost — basic > sums estimatedCost from isMeal activities
computeFoodCost — basic > uses FALLBACK_COST from priceLevel when estimatedCost is absent
computeFoodCost — basic > uses DEFAULT_MEAL_COST (20) when neither estimatedCost nor priceLevel is set
computeFoodCost — basic > FALLBACK_COST tiers: $$$ = 75, $$ = 30, $ = 12
computeFoodCost — basic > computes correct total and avgPerDay across multiple days
computeFoodCost — basic > returns avgPerDay=0 when days array is empty
computeFoodCost — basic > skips non-isMeal activities (regular restaurant activity)
selectHotel — hotel selection > returns PLACEHOLDER hotel when hotels array is empty
selectHotel — hotel selection > returns the single hotel when only one option exists
selectHotel — hotel selection > prefers a hotel in Central/Downtown area (bonus score)
selectHotel — hotel selection > penalises $$$$ hotel when budget < 1500
selectHotel — hotel selection > applies style bonus when hotel tag matches travel style
selectHotel — hotel selection > breaks ties by hotel name (alphabetical)
normalizeDestination > returns 'Top Travel Destination' for vague input 'somewhere'
normalizeDestination > returns 'Top Travel Destination' for all registered vague terms
normalizeDestination > vague match is case-insensitive
normalizeDestination > capitalizes each word
normalizeDestination > preserves all-uppercase acronyms (2–4 letters)
normalizeDestination > normalizes comma spacing — always adds a space after comma
normalizeDestination > collapses multiple spaces
normalizeDestination > trims leading and trailing whitespace
normalizeDestination > handles mixed case correctly
safeDateParsing > returns ISO date string for a valid date
safeDateParsing > returns null for an invalid date string
safeDateParsing > returns null for undefined
safeDateParsing > returns null for empty string
safeDateParsing > parses a natural language date that JS can handle
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > returns a valid TripContext when LLM returns well-formed JSON
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > normalizes destination via normalizeDestination
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > clamps durationDays to [1, 14]
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > uses default 4 days when durationDays is 0 or missing
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > generates days array equal in length to durationDays
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > falls back to generic theme when LLM omits a day
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > de-duplicates day themes
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > normalizes style synonym 'luxurious' to 'luxury'
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > normalizes pace synonym 'easy' to 'slow'
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > drops style if value is invalid (not in VALID_STYLES)
PlannerAgent.run() — happy path > computes endDate from startDate + durationDays when endDate is absent
PlannerAgent.run() — JSON repair > retries with repair prompt when first response is invalid JSON, returns valid result
PlannerAgent.run() — JSON repair > throws AIServiceError with SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED when both LLM calls fail JSON parsing
PlannerAgent.run() — LLM call error propagation > rethrows AIServiceError from the LLM client unchanged
PlannerAgent.run() — LLM call error propagation > wraps generic Error in AIServiceError
PlannerAgent.run() — date fallback > uses a start date ~7 days in the future when LLM provides no dates
ResearchAgent.run() — cache hit > returns cached result immediately without calling the LLM
ResearchAgent.run() — cache hit > returned result has _dataSource from cached groundingMode
ResearchAgent.run() — cache hit > maps groundingMode='unverified' to _dataSource='unverified'
ResearchAgent.run() — cache miss → LLM happy path > returns enriched result with destination and days
ResearchAgent.run() — cache miss → LLM happy path > stores result in cache after successful LLM call
ResearchAgent.run() — cache miss → LLM happy path > attaches geoConfidence to activities from batchGeocode
ResearchAgent.run() — cache miss → LLM happy path > _dataSource is 'brightdata' when Bright Data is enabled
ResearchAgent.run() — cache miss → LLM happy path > _dataSource is 'unverified' when Bright Data is disabled
ResearchAgent.run() — geocoding unavailable > still returns result when centroid geocoding fails (graceful degradation)
ResearchAgent.run() — LLM failure > throws when LLM call fails
SafetyAgent — Rule 1: activity fatigue > no fatigue warning when day has 4 or fewer non-meal activities
SafetyAgent — Rule 1: activity fatigue > emits medium fatigue warning for exactly 5 non-meal activities
SafetyAgent — Rule 1: activity fatigue > emits high fatigue warning for 6 or more non-meal activities
SafetyAgent — Rule 1: activity fatigue > counts only non-meal activities for fatigue
SafetyAgent — Rule 2: long travel gaps > emits no travel warning when travelTimeFromPrevMs is absent
SafetyAgent — Rule 2: long travel gaps > emits medium travel warning for 90-minute travel time
SafetyAgent — Rule 2: long travel gaps > emits high travel warning for 2-hour+ travel time
SafetyAgent — Rule 2: long travel gaps > formats travel time correctly: 1h 30m for 90 minutes
SafetyAgent — Rule 2: long travel gaps > emits one travel warning per long-transit activity
SafetyAgent — Rule 3: late-night schedule > emits no schedule warning when all activities end before 22:00
SafetyAgent — Rule 3: late-night schedule > emits medium schedule warning when activity ends at 22:00 or later
SafetyAgent — Rule 3: late-night schedule > only flags the latest-ending activity per day (no duplicate warnings)
SafetyAgent — Rule 3: late-night schedule > ignores activities with no endTime for schedule rule
SafetyAgent — Rule 4: missing meals > emits no meal warning when isMeal activity is present
SafetyAgent — Rule 4: missing meals > emits medium meal warning when no isMeal activity exists
SafetyAgent — risk level derivation > riskLevel is 'low' when no warnings
SafetyAgent — risk level derivation > riskLevel is 'medium' when only medium-severity warnings exist
SafetyAgent — risk level derivation > riskLevel is 'high' when any high-severity warning is present
SafetyAgent — risk level derivation > multi-day trip: one high-severity day overrides all medium days
SafetyAgent — LLM tips > does NOT call LLM when there are no warnings
SafetyAgent — LLM tips > returns tips from LLM when warnings are present
SafetyAgent — LLM tips > returns empty tips when LLM call fails — warnings are still present
SafetyAgent — LLM tips > caps tips at 4 items
SafetyAgent — LLM tips > filters non-string tips from LLM response
SafetyAgent — combined rules > collects warnings from all four rules simultaneously
SafetyAgent — combined rules > preserves all original context fields in output
serializeRefreshTokenCookie > uses the correct cookie name
serializeRefreshTokenCookie > embeds the token value
serializeRefreshTokenCookie > sets HttpOnly
serializeRefreshTokenCookie > scopes to /api/auth path
clearRefreshTokenCookie > sets MaxAge=0
clearRefreshTokenCookie > uses the same cookie name
clearRefreshTokenCookie > sets empty value
serializeCsrfCookie > uses the CSRF cookie name
serializeCsrfCookie > embeds the token value
serializeCsrfCookie > is NOT HttpOnly (must be JS-readable)
clearCsrfCookie > sets MaxAge=0
clearCsrfCookie > uses the correct cookie name
serializeAccessTokenCookie > uses the correct cookie name
serializeAccessTokenCookie > is HttpOnly
serializeAccessTokenCookie > uses SameSite=Lax
clearAccessTokenCookie > sets MaxAge=0
serializeOAuthStateCookie > uses the OAUTH_STATE_COOKIE name
serializeOAuthStateCookie > embeds the state value
serializeOAuthStateCookie > is HttpOnly
serializeOAuthStateCookie > has a short MaxAge (≤ 600 seconds)
clearOAuthStateCookie > sets MaxAge=0
hashToken > produces a 64-char hex string (SHA-256)
hashToken > is deterministic — same input → same hash
hashToken > different inputs produce different hashes
newTokenFamily > returns a 32-char hex string (16 random bytes)
newTokenFamily > produces unique values on each call
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > signs a token that can be decoded back to the original payload
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > includes a jti field on every signed token
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > jti is unique per token
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > returns a string token
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > throws on a tampered token
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > throws on a completely invalid string
signAccessToken + verifyAccessToken > expires in ~ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRY_MS milliseconds
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > returns an object with rawToken, tokenHash, expiresAt, and jti
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > rawToken is a valid JWT (3-part structure)
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > tokenHash equals hashToken(rawToken)
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > expiresAt is approximately REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRY_MS in the future
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > verifyRefreshToken decodes the raw token correctly
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > two calls produce unique jti values
signRefreshToken + verifyRefreshToken > throws on tampered refresh token
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Tokyo"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Osaka, Japan"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "3 days in tokyo"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "New York City"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "New York"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "London, UK"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Paris"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Seoul"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Singapore"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Hong Kong"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Bangkok"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Mumbai"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Berlin"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Barcelona"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Amsterdam"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Istanbul"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Mexico City"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Kyoto, Japan"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Rome"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Madrid"
isDenseCityDestination — returns true for known dense cities > recognises "Cairo"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Bali"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Maldives"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Iceland"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Tuscany"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Costa Rica"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Patagonia"
isDenseCityDestination — returns false for non-dense destinations > rejects "Queenstown"
isDenseCityDestination — edge cases > is case-insensitive (TOKYO)
isDenseCityDestination — edge cases > handles leading/trailing whitespace
isDenseCityDestination — edge cases > returns false for empty string
isValidGeoCoord — returns true for valid coordinates > Paris (48.8566, 2.3522)
isValidGeoCoord — returns true for valid coordinates > Sydney (-33.8688, 151.2093)
isValidGeoCoord — returns true for valid coordinates > boundary: lat=90, lng=180
isValidGeoCoord — returns true for valid coordinates > boundary: lat=-90, lng=-180
isValidGeoCoord — returns true for valid coordinates > small non-zero (0.001, 0.001)
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > (0, 0) — null island
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > NaN lat
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > NaN lng
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > Infinity lat
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > lat > 90
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > lat < -90
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > lng > 180
isValidGeoCoord — returns false for invalid coordinates > lng < -180
maxDistanceForFeatureType > country type returns 150 km
maxDistanceForFeatureType > region type returns 120 km
maxDistanceForFeatureType > place type (non-dense) returns 50 km
maxDistanceForFeatureType > place type + denseCity=true returns 30 km
maxDistanceForFeatureType > place type + denseCity=false returns 50 km
maxDistanceForFeatureType > country ignores denseCity flag
maxDistanceForFeatureType > region ignores denseCity flag
maxDistanceForFeatureType > dense city threshold (30) < standard city (50) < region (120) < country (150)
getHealingStatus — initial / neutral state > returns active=false when no healing overrides are set
getHealingStatus — initial / neutral state > returns assessment=OK
getHealingStatus — initial / neutral state > returns empty activeActions
getHealingStatus — initial / neutral state > overrides.maxTokensMultiplier is 1.0
getHealingStatus — initial / neutral state > overrides.preferFallbackProvider is false
setHealingOverrides > activates healing and sets assessment
setHealingOverrides > computes maxTokensMultiplier=0.75 for reduce_tokens_25pct
setHealingOverrides > computes maxTokensMultiplier=0.5 for reduce_tokens_50pct
setHealingOverrides > 50pct takes priority over 25pct when both present
setHealingOverrides > sets reduceTimeouts=true for enable_timeout_reduction
setHealingOverrides > filters out no_action and clear_healing from activeActions
setHealingOverrides > increments runCount on each call
setHealingOverrides > sets expiresAt to null when durationMinutes=0
setHealingOverrides > sets expiresAt to a future date when durationMinutes > 0
setHealingOverrides > records triggers and reasoning
clearHealingOverrides > resets active=false and assessment=OK
clearHealingOverrides > resets maxTokensMultiplier to 1.0
clearHealingOverrides > is a no-op when no overrides are active (does not increment runCount)
getHealingStatus — auto-expiry > auto-clears overrides when expiresAt is in the past
applyHealingOverrides > returns config unchanged when no active overrides
applyHealingOverrides > reduces maxTokens by 25% when reduce_tokens_25pct is active
applyHealingOverrides > reduces maxTokens by 50% when reduce_tokens_50pct is active
applyHealingOverrides > clamps reduced maxTokens to minimum 256
applyHealingOverrides > reduces timeoutMs by 30% when enable_timeout_reduction is active
applyHealingOverrides > does NOT mutate the input config
recordRunTimestamps > updates lastRunAt and nextRunAt
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > undefined lat
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > undefined lng
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > both undefined
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > NaN lat
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > NaN lng
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > Infinity lat
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > -Infinity lng
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > (0, 0) — null island
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > lat > 90
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > lat < -90
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > lng > 180
isInvalidCoord — returns true (invalid) for > lng < -180
isInvalidCoord — returns false (valid) for > Paris: (48.8566, 2.3522)
isInvalidCoord — returns false (valid) for > Tokyo: (35.6762, 139.6503)
isInvalidCoord — returns false (valid) for > extreme valid values: lat=90, lng=180
isInvalidCoord — returns false (valid) for > extreme valid values: lat=-90, lng=-180
isInvalidCoord — returns false (valid) for > small but non-zero: (0.001, 0.001)
isInvalidCoord — returns false (valid) for > negative coords (valid southern hemisphere)
haversineDistanceMins — distance calculation > same point returns minimum 5 minutes
haversineDistanceMins — distance calculation > Paris → Eiffel Tower (~2 km) returns a reasonable short time
haversineDistanceMins — distance calculation > London → Paris (~340 km) returns a large time (> 100 min)
haversineDistanceMins — distance calculation > is symmetric: A→B ≈ B→A (within rounding)
haversineDistanceMins — distance calculation > result is always a positive integer (ceil)
haversineDistanceMins — distance calculation > Nairobi → Cape Town (~4000 km) is capped only by downstream logic — raw value is large
getTravelTimeMatrix — fewer than 2 coords > returns [[0]] without usedFallback when coords.length < 2
getTravelTimeMatrix — no Mapbox token (Haversine fallback) > falls back to Haversine when MAPBOX_TOKEN is absent
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > returns the matrix from Mapbox on success
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > fills null cells with Haversine value
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > falls back to Haversine on non-OK HTTP response
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > falls back to Haversine when response has no durations field
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > falls back to Haversine on network fetch error
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > returns cached matrix on Redis hit (new format)
getTravelTimeMatrix — Mapbox fetch succeeds > returns cached matrix on Redis hit (legacy array format)
isAdminPayload — role-based > returns true when role is ADMIN
isAdminPayload — role-based > returns false for role USER with non-admin email
isAdminPayload — role-based > returns false for role MODERATOR with non-admin email
isAdminPayload — email allow-list > returns true for the hardcoded admin@voyageai.com address
isAdminPayload — email allow-list > returns true for the hardcoded suhaas@voyageai.com address
isAdminPayload — email allow-list > matches emails case-insensitively (all caps)
isAdminPayload — email allow-list > matches emails case-insensitively (mixed case)
isAdminPayload — email allow-list > returns false for an address that is not in the allow-list
ADMIN_EMAILS — hardcoded defaults > contains suhaas@voyageai.com
ADMIN_EMAILS — hardcoded defaults > contains admin@voyageai.com
ADMIN_EMAILS — ADMIN_EMAILS env var > includes extra emails from ADMIN_EMAILS (comma-separated)
ADMIN_EMAILS — ADMIN_EMAILS env var > normalizes env-var emails to lowercase
ADMIN_EMAILS — ADMIN_EMAILS env var > still includes hardcoded defaults when env var is set
ADMIN_EMAILS — ADMIN_EMAILS env var > ignores empty and whitespace-only entries in ADMIN_EMAILS
ADMIN_EMAILS — ADMIN_EMAILS env var > handles an unset ADMIN_EMAILS env var gracefully
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=false with a 401 response when unauthenticated
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=false with a 403 response when authenticated but not admin
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=true with auth context when caller has role ADMIN
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=true with auth context when caller is in the email allow-list
requireAdminApiAuth > returns ok=false when email is admin-like but wrong domain
AgentOrchestrator > happy path: returns ok=true when no issues arise
AgentOrchestrator > executes deterministic pipeline in order
AgentOrchestrator > over_budget: LLM chooses reoptimize_budget, loop resolves on second pass
AgentOrchestrator > too_dense: LLM chooses rerun_logistics, loop resolves on second pass
AgentOrchestrator > ask_user: LLM decision triggers immediate human-in-the-loop return
AgentOrchestrator > proceed: LLM decides to proceed even with budget issue, returns ok=true
AgentOrchestrator > exhausted loop: returns requiresHuman after MAX_ITERATIONS (3) unresolved decisions
AgentOrchestrator > exhausted loop: contextual message when both budget and dense
AgentOrchestrator > LLM failure fallback: decision layer throws → defaults to reoptimize_budget
AgentOrchestrator > planner failure: returns ok=false with stage=planner
AgentOrchestrator > research failure: returns ok=false with stage=research
AgentOrchestrator > logistics failure: returns ok=false with stage=logistics
AgentOrchestrator > budget failure in first pass: returns ok=false with stage=budget_safety
AgentOrchestrator > executionLog records agent and llm-decision entries in order
AgentOrchestrator > executionLog is reset on each run() call
AgentOrchestrator > run() return type is OrchestratorResult (type-level check via assignment)
AgentOrchestrator — result shape > happy path returns ok:true with context and executionLog
runViaLangGraph — mocked Python service > returns the LangGraph service response when service is reachable
runViaLangGraph — mocked Python service > falls back to TS orchestrator when Python service is unreachable
runViaLangGraph — mocked Python service > falls back to TS orchestrator when Python service returns non-OK status
runViaLangGraph — mocked Python service > falls back to TS orchestrator when response JSON fails OrchestratorResult validation
runViaLangGraph — mocked Python service > passes request_id in the body
OrchestratorResult structural parity > TS orchestrator ok:true response has all fields expected by LangGraph caller
OrchestratorResult structural parity > TS orchestrator planner-failure response has stage + error fields
runViaLangGraph — live integration (requires Python service) > returns a valid OrchestratorResult from the real Python service
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > returns ok=true with requiresHuman=false
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > calls every agent exactly once
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > passes user input to planner
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > passes planner output to research unchanged
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > passes research output to logistics (hotels present)
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > passes logistics output to budget (selectedHotel set)
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > passes budget output to safety (budget object present)
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > planner output: required fields are defined and valid
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > research output: each day has ≥1 activity and hotels array is non-empty
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > logistics output: selectedHotel defined, every activity has a valid timeSlot
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > budget output: ledger is non-empty, total equals breakdown total
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > final context: destination, dates, and days are intact
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > final context: every day has theme, activities, and no null fields
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > final context: selectedHotel is complete
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > final context: budget is within user budget and ledger is balanced
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > final context: safety result has riskLevel and arrays defined
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > execution log records all 5 agents with status=success in pipeline order
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > no LLM-decision entries in the execution log (clean single pass)
Full itinerary pipeline — end-to-end > no undefined values on critical context fields (null safety check)
brightDataTool — no API key > searchAttractions returns empty string without an API key
brightDataTool — successful responses > returns concatenated title+snippet from organic array
brightDataTool — successful responses > falls back to results array when organic is absent
brightDataTool — successful responses > returns empty string when organic and results are both absent
brightDataTool — successful responses > omits items where both title and body text are empty
brightDataTool — successful responses > truncates output to 2000 characters
brightDataTool — successful responses > sends the API key as a Bearer token in the Authorization header
brightDataTool — error handling > returns failed payload on non-OK HTTP response
brightDataTool — error handling > returns failed payload when fetch throws a network error
testcase.system-out:
brightdata.cache_miss { destination: 'Tokyo', type: 'attraction' }
brightdata.fetch_error { destination: 'Tokyo', type: 'attraction', durationMs: 0 }

testcase.name: brightDataTool — empty result caching > logs brightdata.empty_result when Bright Data returns no items
brightDataTool — timeout retry > retries exactly once on timeout and returns success from second attempt
brightDataTool — stale cache background refresh > returns stale cached data immediately and triggers non-blocking background refresh
testcase.system-out: brightdata.cache_hit { destination: 'Paris', type: 'attraction' } brightdata.stale_refresh_triggered { destination: 'Paris', type: 'attraction' }
